headline: HP’s Closed Loop Copper Push Seeks Edge After Share Price Weakness
summary: HP (NYSE:HPQ) has partnered with Mint Innovation to produce what it calls the first certified batch of closed loop recycled copper from end of life HP printed circuit boards. The certified recycled copper is now being used in new HP laptops, targeting traceability and sustainability gaps in electronics supply chains. This collaboration focuses on creating a repeatable model for circular copper use in PC manufacturing. HP, trading at $18.93, is moving ahead with a sustainability initiative...

headline: Goldman: AI PCs to buck 10% market slump as ‘edge computing’ demand accelerates
summary: Investing.com -- The global personal computer market is bracing for a significant contraction in 2026 as rising component costs and a cooling replacement cycle take their toll on shipments. According to a new report from Goldman Sachs, global PC shipments are now expected to decline by 10% year-on-year in 2026, a steeper drop than previously anticipated, as the industry grapples with the fallout from the Windows 10 "end-of-life" transition.
